title: Creation of Adam
description: Iconic fresco depicting God giving life to Adam, the first man. Part of the Sistine Chapel ceiling, illustrating Genesis 2:7.
attribution: Michelangelo, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Deluge
description: Dramatic depiction of the Great Flood from Genesis, showing humanity overwhelmed by divine judgment as the waters rise.
attribution: Michelangelo, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Fall of Man
description: Michelangelo's Sistine Chapel fresco showing the temptation and expulsion of Adam and Eve from the Garden of Eden.
attribution: Michelangelo, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Sacrifice of Isaac
description: Abraham prepares to sacrifice his son Isaac on Mount Moriah before the angel intervenes, as told in Genesis 22.
attribution: Caravaggio, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Abraham Journeying into the Land of Canaan
description: Abraham leads his family and flocks toward the Promised Land at God's command, the beginning of the covenant journey.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jacob Wrestling with the Angel
description: Jacob wrestles with a mysterious figure through the night at Peniel, receiving the name Israel (Genesis 32).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Joseph Makes Himself Known to His Brethren
description: The emotional moment when Joseph reveals his identity to his brothers in Egypt, a story of forgiveness and providence.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Tower of Babel
description: The ambitious tower reaching toward heaven, scattered by God's confusion of languages (Genesis 11).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Moses Breaking the Tablets of the Law
description: Rembrandt's powerful depiction of Moses shattering the stone tablets upon discovering the Israelites worshipping the golden calf (Exodus 32).
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Finding of Moses
description: Pharaoh's daughter discovers the infant Moses among the reeds of the Nile, a pivotal moment in the Exodus narrative.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Moses and the Burning Bush
description: Moses encounters God in the burning bush on Mount Horeb, receiving his divine commission to free Israel (Exodus 3).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Crossing of the Red Sea
description: The Israelites pass through the parted Red Sea as Pharaoh's army is engulfed by the returning waters (Exodus 14).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Plague of Darkness
description: The ninth plague descends upon Egypt as impenetrable darkness covers the land for three days (Exodus 10:21-23).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Golden Calf
description: The Israelites worship the golden calf while Moses is on Mount Sinai receiving the Law from God (Exodus 32).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Levites Bearing the Ark
description: Doré's engraving of the Levitical priests carrying the Ark of the Covenant through the wilderness, central to Leviticus and Numbers.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Brazen Serpent
description: The Israelites look upon the bronze serpent raised by Moses in the wilderness for healing, as described in Numbers 21:8-9.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Balaam and the Angel
description: The prophet Balaam's donkey sees the angel of the Lord blocking the road, while Balaam himself is blind to the divine messenger (Numbers 22).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Moses Viewing the Promised Land
description: Moses stands on Mount Nebo gazing upon the Promised Land he will never enter, the poignant finale of Deuteronomy.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Death of Moses
description: Moses dies on Mount Nebo after viewing the Promised Land, ending the Pentateuch (Deuteronomy 34).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Walls of Jericho Fall Down
description: The dramatic collapse of Jericho's walls as the Israelites march and blow trumpets, fulfilling God's promise to Joshua.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Joshua Commanding the Sun to Stand Still
description: Joshua commands the sun and moon to stand still during the battle at Gibeon, granting Israel victory (Joshua 10:12-13).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Samson Destroying the Temple
description: Samson brings down the Philistine temple upon himself and his captors, the climax of his story in Judges 16.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Deborah Beneath the Palm Tree
description: The prophetess and judge Deborah holds court beneath her palm tree, leading Israel to victory over the Canaanites (Judges 4).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Gideon Choosing His Soldiers
description: Gideon selects his army by observing how the men drink water from the stream, as commanded by God (Judges 7).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Ruth and Boaz
description: Ruth gleans in the fields of Boaz, a scene of loyalty and providence from the Book of Ruth.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: David and Goliath
description: The young shepherd David faces the Philistine giant Goliath with only a sling and stones, a defining moment of 1 Samuel.
attribution: Caravaggio, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Hannah's Prayer
description: Hannah prays fervently at the tabernacle in Shiloh, vowing to dedicate her son to God's service (1 Samuel 1).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: David Slays Goliath
description: David strikes down the Philistine giant with a stone from his sling, one of the most celebrated victories in scripture.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Anointing of David
description: David is anointed king over Israel, fulfilling the prophecy and the promise of a new era under God's chosen ruler.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Death of Absalom
description: Absalom is caught by his hair in the branches of an oak tree and killed by Joab, ending his rebellion against David (2 Samuel 18).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Judgment of Solomon
description: Raphael's depiction of Solomon's legendary wisdom in resolving the dispute between two mothers claiming the same child (1 Kings 3:16-28).
attribution: Raphael, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Elijah Fed by Ravens
description: The prophet Elijah is sustained by ravens bringing food during the drought at the brook Cherith (1 Kings 17:4-6).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Prophets of Baal Are Slaughtered
description: Elijah's triumph on Mount Carmel as fire from heaven consumes his sacrifice, proving the Lord is God (1 Kings 18).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Elijah Taken Up in a Chariot of Fire
description: The prophet Elijah ascends to heaven in a whirlwind with a chariot of fire, as witnessed by Elisha (2 Kings 2:11).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Naaman Cured of Leprosy
description: The Syrian general Naaman washes seven times in the Jordan River and is healed of his leprosy at Elisha's instruction (2 Kings 5).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: David Brings the Ark to Jerusalem
description: King David leads the joyful procession bringing the Ark of the Covenant into Jerusalem with music and dancing (1 Chronicles 15-16).
attribution: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Destruction of the Temple of Jerusalem
description: Francesco Hayez's painting of the destruction of Solomon's Temple, depicting the catastrophic event recorded in 2 Chronicles.
attribution: Francesco Hayez, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Ezra Reading the Law
description: Doré's engraving of Ezra reading the Book of the Law before the assembled people of Israel after the return from exile.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Nehemiah Views the Ruins of Jerusalem
description: Nehemiah surveys the broken walls of Jerusalem by night, steeling his resolve to rebuild the holy city.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Esther Before the King
description: Queen Esther approaches King Ahasuerus to plead for her people, risking her life in a defining act of courage.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Job and His Friends
description: Job sits in anguish among the ashes while his three friends attempt to comfort him in his suffering.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Job Hearing of His Ruin
description: Job receives the devastating news of the destruction of his family and possessions, testing his faith (Job 1).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: King David Playing the Harp
description: David, the sweet psalmist of Israel, plays his harp in devotion to God — the traditional image of the Psalms' author.
attribution: Gerard van Honthorst, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: King Solomon in Old Age
description: A contemplative portrait of Solomon, traditionally credited as the author of Proverbs, reflecting on wisdom and folly.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Vanitas Still Life
description: A meditation on the transience of earthly pleasures and the inevitability of death, echoing Ecclesiastes' 'Vanity of vanities.'
attribution: Pieter Claesz, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Song of Solomon
description: A lush depiction of the lovers from the Song of Songs, celebrating the beauty of romantic love as described in the biblical poem.
attribution: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Vision of Isaiah
description: Isaiah's prophetic vision of the Lord seated on a high throne with seraphim, as described in Isaiah chapter 6.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Isaiah's Prophecy of the Destruction of Babylon
description: The prophet Isaiah foretells the devastating fall of Babylon, a major prophetic oracle (Isaiah 13-14).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jeremiah Lamenting the Destruction of Jerusalem
description: Rembrandt's haunting portrait of the weeping prophet mourning over Jerusalem's fall, capturing Jeremiah's deep sorrow.
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Destruction of Jerusalem
description: The devastating fall of Jerusalem and its temple, the catastrophe mourned throughout the Book of Lamentations.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Vision of Ezekiel
description: Raphael's masterful rendering of Ezekiel's vision of God enthroned among the four living creatures (Ezekiel 1).
attribution: Raphael, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Valley of Dry Bones
description: Ezekiel's vision of the valley filled with dry bones that are restored to life by the breath of God (Ezekiel 37).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Daniel in the Lions' Den
description: Daniel stands unharmed among the lions, his faith vindicated as God sends an angel to shut the lions' mouths (Daniel 6).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Daniel in the Lions' Den (Rubens)
description: Rubens' dramatic painting of Daniel surrounded by lions in the den, unharmed through God's protection.
attribution: Peter Paul Rubens, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego in the Fiery Furnace
description: The three faithful Hebrews stand unburned in Nebuchadnezzar's fiery furnace, accompanied by a fourth divine figure (Daniel 3).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Belshazzar's Feast
description: The mysterious hand writes on the wall during Belshazzar's feast, foretelling the fall of Babylon (Daniel 5).
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Hosea and Gomer
description: The prophet Hosea's marriage to the unfaithful Gomer, a living parable of God's steadfast love for wayward Israel.
attribution: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Plague of Locusts
description: A devastating swarm of locusts darkens the sky, the central apocalyptic image from the prophecy of Joel.
attribution: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Prophet Amos
description: Amos, the shepherd-prophet from Tekoa, delivers God's message of justice to the nations surrounding Israel.
attribution: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Destruction of Edom
description: The prophesied destruction of Edom for its violence against Israel, the central theme of Obadiah's oracle.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jonah Cast into the Sea
description: Sailors throw the prophet Jonah overboard into the raging storm, where a great fish awaits to swallow him (Jonah 1:15).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jonah Preaching to the Ninevites
description: The prophet Jonah reluctantly preaches to the great city of Nineveh, calling them to repentance (Jonah 3).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Prophet Micah
description: Micah proclaims God's word, prophesying the birthplace of the Messiah in Bethlehem (Micah 5:2).
attribution: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Fall of Nineveh
description: The prophet Nahum's vision of the destruction of Nineveh, capital of the cruel Assyrian Empire, by divine judgment.
attribution: John Martin, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Prophet Habakkuk
description: The prophet Habakkuk wrestles with God over the problem of evil and injustice, crying out for divine answers.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Day of the Lord
description: Zephaniah proclaims the coming Day of the Lord, a time of judgment and purification for Judah and the nations.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Rebuilding of the Temple
description: The prophet Haggai encourages the returned exiles to resume building the second temple in Jerusalem.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Zechariah's Vision of the Lampstand
description: The prophet Zechariah receives visions of the future restoration of Israel, including the golden lampstand (Zechariah 4).
attribution: Julius Schnorr von Carolsfeld, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Messenger of the Covenant
description: Malachi prophesies the coming messenger who will prepare the way before the Lord, the last prophetic voice of the Old Testament.
attribution: Julius Schnorr von Carolsfeld, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Nativity
description: Caravaggio's intimate chiaroscuro painting of the birth of Christ, with the Holy Family bathed in divine light.
attribution: Caravaggio, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Sermon on the Mount
description: Christ delivers the Beatitudes and core teachings to the multitudes gathered on the mountainside (Matthew 5-7).
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Adoration of the Magi
description: The wise men from the East present their gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh to the infant Jesus (Matthew 2).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Flight into Egypt
description: Joseph leads Mary and the infant Jesus on the journey to Egypt, fleeing from Herod's massacre of the innocents (Matthew 2:13-15).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Temptation of Christ
description: Jesus is tempted by Satan in the wilderness after fasting forty days and forty nights (Matthew 4:1-11).
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Transfiguration
description: Jesus is transfigured on the mountain, his face shining like the sun, with Moses and Elijah appearing beside him (Matthew 17).
attribution: Raphael, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jesus Entering Jerusalem
description: Christ's triumphal entry into Jerusalem on Palm Sunday, greeted by crowds waving palm branches (Matthew 21).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Crucifixion (Matthew)
description: The crucifixion of Christ at Golgotha with darkness covering the land, the culminating event of Matthew's passion narrative.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Baptism of Christ
description: John the Baptist baptizes Jesus in the Jordan River as the Holy Spirit descends like a dove (Mark 1:9-11).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jesus Healing the Sick
description: Jesus heals the multitudes who come to him, demonstrating his compassion and divine power over illness.
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jesus Calming the Storm
description: Jesus rebukes the wind and waves, calming the stormy Sea of Galilee while his terrified disciples watch in awe (Mark 4:35-41).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Feeding of the Five Thousand
description: Jesus multiplies five loaves and two fish to feed a multitude of five thousand people in the wilderness (Mark 6:30-44).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jesus Walking on Water
description: Jesus walks on the Sea of Galilee toward his disciples' boat during a storm, calling Peter to come to him (Mark 6:45-52).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Prodigal Son
description: Rembrandt's masterpiece depicting the father embracing his lost son who has returned home, illustrating Luke 15:11-32.
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Annunciation
description: The angel Gabriel announces to Mary that she will bear the Son of God, the opening scene of Luke's Gospel.
attribution: Raphael, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Good Samaritan
description: The parable of the Good Samaritan who stops to help a wounded stranger on the road from Jerusalem to Jericho (Luke 10:25-37).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Nativity (Luke)
description: The shepherds visit the newborn Jesus lying in a manger in Bethlehem, as announced by the angels (Luke 2).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jesus Among the Doctors
description: The twelve-year-old Jesus astonishes the teachers in the temple with his understanding and answers (Luke 2:41-52).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Resurrection
description: The risen Christ emerges from the tomb, the central event of Christian faith as described in Luke 24.
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Last Supper
description: Leonardo da Vinci's iconic depiction of Jesus sharing bread and wine with his disciples on the night before his crucifixion (John 13).
attribution: Leonardo da Vinci, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Crucifixion
description: Raphael's rendering of the crucifixion of Christ, the central event of the Gospel narrative described in John 19.
attribution: Raphael, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Raising of Lazarus
description: Jesus calls Lazarus forth from the tomb after four days, one of the greatest miracles recorded in John's Gospel (John 11).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Woman at the Well
description: Jesus speaks with the Samaritan woman at Jacob's well, revealing himself as the source of living water (John 4).
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Doubting Thomas
description: The risen Jesus invites Thomas to touch his wounds, leading to Thomas's confession 'My Lord and my God!' (John 20:24-29).
attribution: Caravaggio, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Christ Washing the Disciples' Feet
description: Jesus humbly washes his disciples' feet, demonstrating servant leadership on the night before his crucifixion (John 13:1-17).
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Christ in Gethsemane
description: Jesus prays in agony in the Garden of Gethsemane on the night of his arrest, sweating drops of blood (John 18; Luke 22).
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Conversion of Saint Paul
description: Caravaggio's dramatic depiction of Saul struck down by divine light on the road to Damascus, his conversion moment (Acts 9).
attribution: Caravaggio, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Descent of the Holy Spirit at Pentecost
description: The Holy Spirit descends upon the apostles as tongues of fire on the day of Pentecost, empowering them to preach (Acts 2).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Stoning of Stephen
description: Stephen, the first Christian martyr, is stoned to death while praying for his persecutors (Acts 7:54-60).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Paul Preaching at Athens
description: Paul addresses the Athenian philosophers at the Areopagus, proclaiming the unknown God (Acts 17:22-34).
attribution: Raphael, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Paul's Shipwreck on Malta
description: Paul's ship is wrecked in a storm on the way to Rome, and the survivors escape to the island of Malta (Acts 27-28).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Paul Writing His Epistles
description: Rembrandt's contemplative portrait of the Apostle Paul composing his letters, including his Epistle to the Romans.
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Paul Preaching in Athens
description: Raphael's tapestry cartoon of Paul addressing the Athenian philosophers on Mars Hill about the 'unknown god' (Acts 17).
attribution: Raphael, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Paul (2 Corinthians)
description: Paul the Apostle, depicted in contemplation as he writes his second letter to the Corinthian church, defending his apostleship.
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Paul and the Epistle to the Galatians
description: Paul writes passionately to the Galatians about justification by faith, not by works of the law.
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Paul in Prison
description: Paul writes from prison to the Ephesian church about the mystery of Christ and the unity of all believers in one body.
attribution: Rembrandt (attributed), Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Paul Writing to the Philippians
description: Paul writes his letter of joy to the Philippian church from imprisonment, encouraging them to rejoice in the Lord always.
attribution: Valentin de Boulogne, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Paul Writing to the Colossians
description: Paul writes to the church at Colossae about the supremacy of Christ over all creation and all spiritual powers.
attribution: Valentin de Boulogne, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Paul and the Thessalonians
description: Paul writes to the church at Thessalonica about the second coming of Christ and encouragement for faithful living.
attribution: Valentin de Boulogne, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Second Coming
description: Paul writes his second letter to the Thessalonians about the Day of the Lord and the man of lawlessness.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Paul Writing to Timothy
description: Paul writes to his young protege Timothy, giving instructions for church leadership and sound doctrine.
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Paul's Final Letter to Timothy
description: Paul writes his final epistle from prison to Timothy, passing the torch of ministry before his expected martyrdom.
attribution: Rembrandt (attributed), Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Paul's Letter to Titus
description: Paul instructs Titus on appointing elders and maintaining sound teaching in the churches of Crete.
attribution: Valentin de Boulogne, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Paul's Letter to Philemon
description: Paul appeals to Philemon on behalf of the runaway slave Onesimus, urging forgiveness and brotherhood in Christ.
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Melchizedek Blesses Abraham
description: The priest-king Melchizedek blesses Abraham, a key typological figure discussed at length in the Epistle to the Hebrews.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Hall of Faith
description: Abraham sets out in faith toward an unknown land, exemplifying the faith chapter of Hebrews 11.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint James the Apostle
description: James, the brother of Jesus, who wrote the epistle calling for faith demonstrated through works and practical holiness.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Peter Preaching
description: Peter preaches to the early church, embodying the pastoral leadership reflected in his first epistle.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Peter's Warning
description: Peter warns the early church against false teachers and reminds them of the coming Day of the Lord in his second epistle.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint John the Evangelist on Patmos
description: The apostle John on the island of Patmos, author of the epistles proclaiming that God is love and light.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint John's Second Epistle
description: John writes a brief letter to the 'chosen lady' and her children, urging them to walk in truth and love.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint John's Third Epistle
description: John writes to Gaius commending his hospitality toward traveling missionaries and warning against the controlling Diotrephes.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Epistle of Jude
description: Jude warns against ungodly people who have crept into the church, urging believers to contend for the faith once delivered to the saints.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse
description: Dürer's iconic woodcut of the four horsemen riding forth — Conquest, War, Famine, and Death — from Revelation 6.
attribution: Albrecht Dürer, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The New Jerusalem
description: A glorious vision of the heavenly city descending from God, the culmination of biblical prophecy in Revelation 21.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint John's Vision on Patmos
description: The apostle John receives the apocalyptic visions on the island of Patmos, beginning the Book of Revelation (Revelation 1).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Whore of Babylon
description: The great harlot seated on a scarlet beast, representing the corrupt worldly power destined for judgment (Revelation 17).
attribution: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Last Judgment
description: Michelangelo's monumental fresco of the final judgment, when Christ separates the righteous from the wicked (Revelation 20).
attribution: Michelangelo, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Separation of Light from Darkness
description: Michelangelo's Sistine Chapel panel showing God creating light and separating it from darkness on the first day.
attribution: Michelangelo, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Lot Fleeing from Sodom
description: Lot and his family flee the destruction of Sodom by fire and brimstone as his wife looks back and becomes a pillar of salt (Genesis 19).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Noah's Ark
description: The animals enter Noah's ark in pairs before the great flood covers the earth (Genesis 7).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Moses Receives the Law on Sinai
description: Moses receives the Ten Commandments from God on Mount Sinai amid thunder, lightning, and smoke (Exodus 19-20).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Destruction of Pharaoh's Army
description: The waters of the Red Sea close over Pharaoh's chariots and horsemen as Israel watches from the far shore (Exodus 14-15).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Stoning of Achan
description: Achan is punished for taking forbidden spoils from Jericho, a lesson in obedience from Joshua 7.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jephthah's Daughter
description: Jephthah's daughter comes out to meet her father after his tragic vow, a poignant story from Judges 11.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Samson and Delilah
description: Delilah betrays Samson by having his hair cut while he sleeps, robbing him of his God-given strength (Judges 16).
attribution: Peter Paul Rubens, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saul Attacks David
description: King Saul hurls a javelin at David as the young musician plays the harp, driven by jealousy and an evil spirit (1 Samuel 18-19).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Witch of Endor
description: King Saul consults the medium at Endor and encounters the spirit of Samuel before his final defeat (1 Samuel 28).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: David's Lament for Absalom
description: King David weeps bitterly upon hearing of the death of his rebellious son Absalom: 'O my son Absalom!' (2 Samuel 19).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Solomon's Temple Dedication
description: King Solomon dedicates the newly completed Temple in Jerusalem, as the glory of the Lord fills the house (1 Kings 8).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Destruction of Sennacherib's Army
description: The angel of the Lord strikes down 185,000 Assyrian soldiers besieging Jerusalem in a single night (2 Kings 19:35).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: David's Psalm of Praise
description: David raises his voice in thanksgiving to God, the archetypal psalmist whose songs define the Book of Psalms.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Calling of Isaiah
description: A seraph touches Isaiah's lips with a burning coal from the altar, purifying him for prophetic ministry (Isaiah 6:6-7).
attribution: Julius Schnorr von Carolsfeld, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jeremiah in the Pit
description: The prophet Jeremiah is lowered into a muddy cistern by his enemies for prophesying Jerusalem's fall (Jeremiah 38).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Ascension of Christ
description: The risen Christ ascends to heaven from the Mount of Olives as his disciples watch in wonder (Acts 1:9-11).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Peter Delivered from Prison
description: An angel appears in Peter's prison cell and miraculously frees him from his chains and guards (Acts 12).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Agony in the Garden
description: Jesus prays in anguish at Gethsemane while his disciples sleep, on the night before his crucifixion (Mark 14:32-42).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Arrest of Jesus
description: Judas betrays Jesus with a kiss as soldiers arrive in the Garden of Gethsemane to arrest him (Mark 14:43-52).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Burial of Christ
description: The body of Jesus is placed in the tomb of Joseph of Arimathea after the crucifixion (Mark 15:42-47).
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Christ and the Rich Young Ruler
description: Jesus tells the rich young man to sell all he has and give to the poor, a challenging encounter (Mark 10:17-27).
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jesus and the Woman Taken in Adultery
description: Jesus stoops to write in the dust as the Pharisees present a woman caught in adultery, then declares 'Let him who is without sin cast the first stone' (John 8:1-11).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Supper at Emmaus
description: The risen Jesus reveals himself to two disciples at Emmaus in the breaking of bread (Luke 24:13-35).
attribution: Caravaggio, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Manna from Heaven
description: God provides manna from heaven to feed the Israelites during their wilderness journey (Exodus 16).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Twelve Spies Return from Canaan
description: The spies return from scouting the Promised Land carrying an enormous cluster of grapes from Eshcol (Numbers 13).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Samson Slaying a Lion
description: The young Samson tears apart a lion with his bare hands, empowered by the Spirit of the Lord (Judges 14:5-6).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Ruth and Naomi
description: Ruth clings to her mother-in-law Naomi, declaring 'Where you go I will go' — one of the Bible's most famous pledges of loyalty (Ruth 1:16).
attribution: Julius Schnorr von Carolsfeld, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Calling of Samuel
description: The young Samuel hears God calling his name in the night at the tabernacle in Shiloh (1 Samuel 3).
attribution: Julius Schnorr von Carolsfeld, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Bathsheba at Her Bath
description: King David observes Bathsheba bathing from his rooftop, the beginning of his great sin (2 Samuel 11).
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Queen of Sheba Visits Solomon
description: The Queen of Sheba arrives in Jerusalem with gifts and questions for King Solomon, marveling at his wisdom (1 Kings 10).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Destruction of Leviathan
description: God's power over the great sea monster Leviathan, described in Job 41 as an unstoppable creature under divine authority.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Fiery Flying Serpent
description: Isaiah's vision of seraphim and fiery creatures, connected to the prophet's dramatic call narrative.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Parable of the Ten Virgins
description: Five wise and five foolish virgins await the bridegroom, a parable of readiness for Christ's return (Matthew 25:1-13).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jesus Cleansing the Temple
description: Jesus drives out the money changers and merchants from the temple courts in holy indignation (Matthew 21:12-13).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Christ Before Pilate
description: Jesus stands before Pontius Pilate, who finds no guilt in him but yields to the crowd's demands (Matthew 27:11-26).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Hagar and Ishmael in the Wilderness
description: Hagar and her son Ishmael wander in the wilderness of Beersheba after being sent away by Abraham (Genesis 21:14-19).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Joseph Sold by His Brothers
description: Joseph's jealous brothers sell him to Ishmaelite traders heading to Egypt, beginning his long exile (Genesis 37).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Burning of Jerusalem
description: The city of Jerusalem burns as the Babylonians complete their destruction, the event that occasioned the Book of Lamentations.
attribution: Julius Schnorr von Carolsfeld, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Ezekiel's Vision of the New Temple
description: Ezekiel sees the vision of the restored and glorified temple of God, described in detail in Ezekiel 40-48.
attribution: Julius Schnorr von Carolsfeld, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Writing on the Wall
description: A mysterious hand writes the words MENE, MENE, TEKEL, UPHARSIN on the wall during Belshazzar's feast (Daniel 5).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Daniel's Vision of the Four Beasts
description: Daniel's apocalyptic vision of four great beasts representing successive world empires (Daniel 7).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Healing of the Blind Man
description: Jesus heals a man born blind by making clay with his saliva and applying it to the man's eyes (John 9).
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Visitation
description: Mary visits her cousin Elizabeth, who is pregnant with John the Baptist. Elizabeth greets Mary with the words 'Blessed are you among women' (Luke 1:39-56).
attribution: Raphael, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Presentation at the Temple
description: Simeon holds the infant Jesus in the temple and declares him a light for revelation to the Gentiles (Luke 2:22-35).
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Parable of the Sower
description: Jesus teaches the crowd the parable of the sower and the four types of soil, representing different responses to God's word (Luke 8:4-15).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Cain Slaying Abel
description: Cain murders his brother Abel in the first act of violence in human history, driven by jealousy over God's acceptance of Abel's offering (Genesis 4).
attribution: Peter Paul Rubens, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Expulsion from Eden
description: Adam and Eve are driven out of the Garden of Eden by an angel with a flaming sword (Genesis 3:22-24).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Crowning with Thorns
description: Roman soldiers mock Jesus with a crown of thorns and a purple robe before his crucifixion (John 19:1-5).
attribution: Caravaggio, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Noli Me Tangere
description: The risen Jesus appears to Mary Magdalene at the tomb and tells her 'Do not touch me, for I have not yet ascended' (John 20:17).
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Philip and the Ethiopian Eunuch
description: Philip explains the scriptures to the Ethiopian official and baptizes him on the road from Jerusalem to Gaza (Acts 8:26-40).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Destruction of Sodom
description: Fire and brimstone rain down upon the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah as divine judgment for their wickedness (Genesis 19).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Seven Seals
description: The Lamb of God opens the seven seals of judgment, unleashing divine wrath upon the earth (Revelation 5-8).
attribution: Albrecht Dürer, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Angel with the Key of the Abyss
description: An angel descends from heaven with the key to the bottomless pit and a great chain to bind Satan (Revelation 20:1-3).
attribution: Albrecht Dürer, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Sacrifice of Noah
description: Noah offers a sacrifice of thanksgiving after the flood, and God establishes his covenant with the rainbow (Genesis 8:20-22).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Isaac Blessing Jacob
description: The blind Isaac is deceived into blessing his younger son Jacob instead of Esau (Genesis 27).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jacob's Dream
description: Jacob sleeps at Bethel and dreams of a ladder reaching to heaven with angels ascending and descending (Genesis 28:10-22).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Joseph Interprets Pharaoh's Dream
description: Joseph interprets Pharaoh's dreams of seven fat cows and seven lean cows, foretelling years of plenty and famine (Genesis 41).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Firstborn Slain
description: The angel of death passes over Egypt, striking down every firstborn in the final and most devastating plague (Exodus 12).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Water from the Rock
description: Moses strikes the rock at Horeb and water flows out for the thirsty Israelites in the wilderness (Exodus 17:1-7).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Rebellion of Korah
description: The earth opens up and swallows Korah and his followers who rebelled against Moses' authority (Numbers 16).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Christ Healing the Paralytic at Bethesda
description: Jesus heals the paralyzed man at the pool of Bethesda who had waited 38 years (John 5:1-15).
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Calling of Matthew
description: Jesus calls the tax collector Matthew (Levi) to follow him, as depicted in Caravaggio's masterful use of light and shadow (Matthew 9:9).
attribution: Caravaggio, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Tribute Money
description: Jesus instructs Peter to find a coin in a fish's mouth to pay the temple tax, a scene from Matthew 17:24-27.
attribution: Peter Paul Rubens, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Ecce Homo
description: Pilate presents the scourged Jesus to the crowd with the words 'Behold the man' before handing him over (John 19:5).
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Massacre of the Innocents
description: King Herod orders the massacre of all male infants in Bethlehem in his attempt to destroy the newborn King (Matthew 2:16-18).
attribution: Peter Paul Rubens, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Descent from the Cross
description: Christ's body is lowered from the cross by his followers, a scene of profound grief and tenderness.
attribution: Peter Paul Rubens, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Elisha and the Bears
description: Two bears come out of the woods and maul the youths who had mocked the prophet Elisha (2 Kings 2:23-25).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Moses Before Pharaoh
description: Moses and Aaron confront Pharaoh demanding the release of the Israelites, as the plagues begin (Exodus 7-10).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Judgment of Solomon (Doré)
description: King Solomon reveals his divine wisdom by resolving the dispute of two mothers who claim the same child (1 Kings 3).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Levitical Priests Offering Sacrifice
description: The Levitical priests perform the sacrificial rituals described in the Book of Leviticus at the tabernacle altar.
attribution: Julius Schnorr von Carolsfeld, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Moses' Final Address
description: Moses delivers his final speeches to the Israelites, recounting God's laws before they enter the Promised Land.
attribution: Julius Schnorr von Carolsfeld, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons
